title: Telemarketing Bureaus and Other Contact Centers

narrative.executive_view: This is a technically exposed but economically awkward AI target. NAICS 561422 is specifically outsourced communication performed for clients, not the client's in-house contact center and not telephone answering services [S1]. Routine calls, chats, summaries, routing and quality review are increasingly automatable, but the operator usually sells the labor-linked unit that AI removes. Current CX contract research says more than 70% of primary contracts remain unit-based or time-and-materials while only 5% are outcome/gainshare [S5]. The base case is therefore high replaceable labor, weak retained savings and melting terminal demand for undifferentiated human-seat providers.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: Customer-service AI has moved quickly from experiment toward deployment: a 2026 global survey reports 66% of service organizations use agentic AI, though that is a broad, vendor-sponsored measure rather than a 561422 statistic [S3]. Voice remains less mature: Gartner's 2024 survey found 5% deployed and 11% piloting customer-facing GenAI voicebots [S2]. The likely operating model is autonomous handling of routine intents and outbound scripts, AI assistance for complex contacts, automated summaries and QA, and a smaller human tier for exceptions, empathy, regulated disclosures and difficult collections. Cisco's forecast of 68% agentic handling by 2028 supports the direction but also finds strong demand for retained human connection [S4].
narrative.value_capture: The central problem is commercial, not technical. Legacy contracts monetize minutes, hours, seats, calls or transactions, so fewer human interactions can mean fewer billed units. HFS reports 36% unit-based and 36% time-and-materials primary CX contracts, versus only 5% outcome/gainshare [S5]. Large operators can redesign around outcomes—Concentrix now markets an outcome-based managed service [S6]—but that capability is itself evidence that buyers can demand the savings. A subscale bureau without proprietary data, integrations or regulated workflow depth should expect substantial pass-through and direct competition from CCaaS and AI vendors.
narrative.adoption_timing: Effective replacement is estimated at 30% today, below the much broader 66% organizational-use statistic because tool adoption includes agent assist, routing and internal workflows that do not remove a seat [S3]. Voice deployment data started much lower in 2024 [S2], while three-year interaction forecasts are aggressive [S4]. A 2.5-year path to 50% effective adoption is therefore plausible, with a 1.5-4 year range driven by data integration, accuracy, consent and sector-specific compliance. The estimate applies to outsourced workflows; in-house buyer adoption can bypass the bureau and may move on a different clock.
narrative.consolidation_economics: There is an active strategic market, but public precedents are much larger and more diversified than the target band. A 2025 SEC-filed transaction table shows four recent CX transactions with distinct buyers and a 6.8x median adjusted-EBITDA multiple, down from a 10.2x pre-AI median [S7]. Concentrix described its Webhelp price as about 9.5x anticipated EBITDA and 7.6x after expected synergies, a large-platform ceiling rather than a small-bureau comp [S8]. Generic small-deal evidence clusters around 5.5-5.6x, with somewhat higher multiples above that tier [S9]. I use 5.5x buy and 6.8x exit, but client concentration, offshore exposure and AI-driven volume loss could erase the modeled spread.
narrative.terminal_demand: Undifferentiated outsourced human conversation is a melting product because the buyer owns the brand, customer data and reason for contact, while the bureau performs communications for that buyer [S1]. Mature AI can perform routine interactions directly; the surviving paid work is escalations, sensitive or regulated interactions, complex retention and human-supervised AI operations. Cisco's research supports a human residual even under high automation [S4], but that residual should carry lower total volume and intense price competition. Terminal demand could be preserved only if the operator becomes an outcome-owning, integrated workflow provider rather than remaining a seat vendor.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The largest evidence mismatch is population: adoption surveys cover global customer-service organizations, including in-house centers, whereas NAICS 561422 covers U.S. establishments communicating for others [S1-S4]. The supplied occupation mix is a 4-digit 5614 proxy that includes sibling-code artifacts and understates frontline agents at the 6-digit industry. Ownership age is a generic employer-business proxy [S10], and the multiple evidence mixes giant strategic deals with generic small transactions [S7-S9]. Offshore delivery is outside U.S. NAICS data, so observed domestic contraction can reflect offshoring as well as AI. These limitations make exact timing, capture and entry valuation more uncertain than the direction of task automation.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition: 561422 Telemarketing Bureaus and Other Contact Centers
sources.what_it_supports: Defines the industry as call/contact centers initiating or receiving communications for others and distinguishes the 561421 telephone-answering sibling.

sources.title: Gartner Survey Reveals 85% of Customer Service Leaders Will Explore or Pilot Customer-Facing Conversational GenAI in 2025
sources.what_it_supports: A 2024 survey of 187 service leaders found 5% deployed, 11% piloting and 44% exploring a customer-facing GenAI voicebot.

sources.title: New Research: AI Service Agents Are Scaling and Delivering CSAT
sources.what_it_supports: Salesforce's March-April 2026 global survey of 3,075 service professionals reports agentic-AI use rising from 39% to 66%, with 77% of adopters using it in both customer-facing and internal operations.

sources.title: Agentic AI Poised to Handle 68% of Customer Service and Support Interactions by 2028
sources.what_it_supports: A global technology-buyer survey forecasts 68% agentic handling within three years while reporting strong preference for retaining human connection.

sources.title: CX buyers want outcome-based pricing, yet their contracts still pay for effort
sources.what_it_supports: HFS's July 2026 CX research reports primary contracts at 36% unit-based, 36% time-and-materials, 13% hybrid and only 5% outcome-based or gainshare.

sources.title: Concentrix Next Generation Customer Care
sources.what_it_supports: Shows a major CX outsourcer commercializing AI-enabled managed service through value- and outcome-based pricing.

sources.title: SEC-filed customer-experience precedent transaction analysis
sources.what_it_supports: Lists four 2023-2024 CX transactions with distinct acquirers and reports a 6.8x median adjusted-EBITDA multiple versus 10.2x for older precedents.

sources.title: Concentrix-Webhelp transaction conference call
sources.what_it_supports: Concentrix stated it paid about 9.5x anticipated 2023 adjusted EBITDA and described a 7.6x multiple after expected run-rate synergies.

sources.title: Small-Deal Resilience: Why the Under $25 Million Tier Still Moves in H1 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Generic private M&A evidence: the smallest tiers averaged about 5.5-5.6x TTM EBITDA and the $10-25 million tier averaged 6.2-6.7x.

sources.title: Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan
sources.what_it_supports: Cites Census data that 52.3% of U.S. employer businesses are owned by people 55 or older and reports broad succession-plan evidence; it is not 561422-specific.

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 561422 ($17,861,129,852) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 561422 ($32,736,522,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

occupations.title: First-Line Supervisors of Office and Administrative Support Workers
Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
Mail Clerks and Mail Machine Operators, Except Postal Service

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[56]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $84,840 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 561422); class <5: 926 firms, avg revenue/firm $563,971 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $88,261 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 247 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,328,636 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $207,931 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 210 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,113,986 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $487,339 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 400 firms, avg revenue/firm $5,157,090 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $807,085 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 201 firms, avg revenue/firm $24,411,512 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,820,402 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 315 firms, avg revenue/firm $77,024,206 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $12,054,288 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 201
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: Routine customer service, telemarketing, collections contacts, QA, summaries and scheduling are increasingly automatable, while exception handling, empathy, regulated disclosures, enterprise selling and AI operations persist. Fractions are applied to the supplied 4-digit roles and deliberately remain below the most aggressive interaction forecasts.
caveats: The source surveys cover broad global or in-house customer-service populations, not U.S. NAICS 561422 establishments.
The supplied 4-digit 5614 occupation mix contains sibling-code artifacts and likely understates frontline-agent weight at 561422.
Interaction automation is not identical to wage removal; retained humans handle exceptions and supervise AI.

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Routine status, account, order and FAQ contacts can be handled end-to-end; complex, distressed and policy-exception contacts remain human.
Scripted outbound qualification and promotion are highly automatable, with consent, persuasion and exception handling limiting full replacement.
Reminders, payment links and routine negotiation can automate, but disputes, hardship and regulated collection interactions retain humans.

breakdown_by_role.role: First-Line Supervisors of Office and Administrative Support Workers
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Automated QA, forecasting and coaching widen spans, while escalation ownership and people management persist.

breakdown_by_role.role: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Proposal drafting and lead research automate, but enterprise RFP selling and relationship ownership remain human.
Reporting and planning automate partially; client, regulatory and operating accountability remain.
This is likely a sibling-code proxy artifact; where analogous routine counter or account transactions exist, most tasks automate.
Transcription is directly exposed to speech recognition and generative cleanup, subject to human QA.

breakdown_by_role.role: Mail Clerks and Mail Machine Operators, Except Postal Service
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Routine mail and document routing can digitize or automate; physical exceptions remain.
Tier-one desktop support automates, while platform integration, incident response and AI operations grow.
The large residual likely includes additional frontline, clerical and technical roles at the 6-digit industry; use a bounded blended fraction rather than assuming all are agents.

pi_dist.rationale: Most contracts still monetize units or effort, so automation removes billed volume; outcome pricing can retain more value but represents only a small observed share and is more credible for scaled integrators.
caveats: HFS covers enterprise CX contracts, not only domestic lower-middle-market bureaus.
Concentrix demonstrates feasibility for a global integrator, not transferability to a subscale operator.

pi_moat.rationale: A bureau can retain value through workflow integration, compliance and outcome accountability, but the NAICS product is communication performed for clients and the buyer can procure AI or CCaaS directly.
caveats: Moat depends heavily on vertical workflow depth and client-owned data access; the 6-digit definition does not measure either.

t50_years.rationale: Voice deployment began from a low base, but broader agentic adoption accelerated sharply and customers forecast majority interaction handling within three years; 50% effective labor replacement should lag tool use.
caveats: Forecast and adoption sources are broad and vendor-sponsored; they are not a longitudinal 561422 measure.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Effective replacement is set well below reported organizational agentic-AI use because much deployment is agent assist or internal automation and voice deployment started lower.
caveats: This is effective task adoption, not the percentage of organizations that have any AI.
The population includes in-house contact centers outside NAICS 561422.

historical_analogs.value: IVR/self-service and offshore BPO first removed simple contacts and repriced labor while leaving complex contacts to humans; agentic AI extends that substitution into natural-language handling and back-office actions.
historical_analogs.rationale: The analogy explains staged substitution and a residual human tier, but the sources describe the current transition rather than a clean historical time series.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: Faster than offshore migration for digital channels; similar multi-year enterprise integration for regulated voice
caveats: No source provides a directly comparable 561422 historical diffusion curve.

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: Generic Census-linked evidence says 52.3% of employer businesses have owners age 55 or older; mapping that threshold to age 60+ yields a conservative 35% estimate.
caveats: Generic U.S. employer-business population; no 561422 owner-age table was found.
The source threshold is 55+, while the scored input is 60+.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: Broad succession-planning evidence exists, but no contact-center-specific ownership succession shortage was identified, so the industry condition is not marked documented.
caveats: Generic succession evidence cannot establish an industry-specific shortage.

active_consolidators.rationale: The SEC-filed recent-transaction set identifies four distinct active acquirers: Founder & Group, Capital Square Partners, Teleperformance and Concentrix.
caveats: The transactions are large global CX platforms, not necessarily buyers of U.S. $1-10M EBITDA pure-play bureaus.

buy_mult.rationale: Use the bottom of observed private-market ranges because the target is smaller, concentrated and directly exposed to AI, despite a 6.8x recent large-sector median.
caveats: GF Data tiers are organized by enterprise value, not the prompt's $1-10M EBITDA band.
Sector precedents are much larger and more diversified than the target.

exit_mult.rationale: The selected exit equals the recent large-sector transaction median and sits near generic upper-small-deal ranges; it assumes a scaled, diversified operator despite AI pressure.
caveats: The 6.8x observation is a median of four large transactions and is not a direct lower-middle-market exit comp.
Webhelp's higher multiple included scale and expected synergies.

pricing_structure.value: Predominantly per-unit and effort pricing (seats, time, interactions or transactions), with outcome/gainshare still rare but being introduced by scaled integrators.
pricing_structure.rationale: HFS quantifies current primary contract structures and Concentrix provides a live outcome-priced counterexample.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: Legacy input/unit pricing remains the majority; outcome pricing 5% observed with a higher future share
caveats: Enterprise contract mix may differ from small domestic bureau contracts.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: The large technical wage opportunity is consistent with a 2.5-year t50: value exists, but adoption is already material and progressing quickly rather than being distant.

terminal_value.justification: The undifferentiated product is human communication sold on behalf of a client that can deploy AI directly. A smaller high-complexity human tier survives, but majority seat-based demand and pricing units erode.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No. Adoption evidence includes in-house and global customer-service populations; the role mix is 4-digit 5614; owner age and small-deal multiples are broad proxies. Each is disclosed on the affected input.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Explicitly reflected in pi_dist: under unit/time/FTE pricing, deflection and shorter handle time remove billable units; only outcome pricing offers a credible route to retain part of the savings.

dataset_mismatch.description: The supplied OEWS role mix is 4-digit 5614 rather than 6-digit 561422 and contains occupations associated with sibling business-support codes; the residual is therefore material.
sensitivity: A true 561422 mix with 55-70% frontline-agent wage share would likely place ai_replaceable_share around 0.55-0.65.
If the residual is primarily technical/client-management work rather than agents, ai_replaceable_share could fall toward 0.48.

subsegments: offshore delivery outside U.S. NAICS counts
market_boundary.dominant_subsegment: outsourced inbound and outbound client contact operations
market_boundary.selection_basis: Census defines 561422 as communications performed for others. Score estimates use broad contact-center evidence only as a disclosed proxy and exclude in-house centers from the formal industry boundary.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: Moves A from about 6.7 to 2.5 before any gate effects.
With pi_moat fixed at 0.30, moves C from 0.45 to 1.20.
Changes M materially and could eliminate the assumed exit spread at the high end.

reviewer_note: Every registered URL was reopened on 2026-07-21. The TTEC filing surfaced during search but was excluded after its live reopen failed; no claim depends on it.

A.arithmetic: Python v3.1.2: A = 10 when t50=0 else min(10,10/t50); t50=2.5, A=4
